Farx P or-rrrcar- PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
October 5,2001
Valerie Burrowes, CMCA4MC
Sacramento City Clerk
915 I Street, Room 301
Sacramento, CA 9581 4-2671
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-01-243
Dear Ms. Burrowes:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTION
You have requested approval for City of Sacramento candidates and committees
to use the modified Form 460 approved for use by the City and County of San Francisco,
to comply with the campaign reporting provisions of the Act.
CONCLUSION
The Form 460 is approved for use by city of Sacramento candidates and
committees
FACTS
The Form 460 (Recipient Committee Campaign Statement) was revised to
implement legislative changes that were signed into law last year (Chapter 853, Stats.
2000), as well as Proposition34, which was passed by the voters in the November 2000
general election.
I Govemment Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
'g


Our File No. ,{-01-231
Page2
The San Francisco Ethics Commission ("Ethics Commission") established an
electronic campaign filing program for city and county committees. The program also
allows filers to print and file paper copies of campaign statements that comply with the
campaign disclosure requirements of the Act. The City of Sacramento uses the online
filing software developed by the Ethics Commission for electronic filing by city
candidates and committees. Due to time and budget constraints, the Ethics Commission
will not be able to revise its print software to enable filers to print out the new version of
Form 460 by the October 70,2001filing deadline.
You have requested approval for City of Sacramento candidates and committees
to use the same modified Form 460 approved for use by City and County of San
Francisco candidates and committees. The modified Form 460 includes all of the
information required for local candidates and committees by the revised Form 460
approved by the Fair Political Practices Commission on June 8, 2001. Filers will print
the Form 460 currently generated by the city's electronic filing system; those that have
loan information to report will attach the new Form 460 Schedule B andlor H, as
appropriate, with their paper filings.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates and committees to file periodic campaign statements
disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with state and
local elections. ($ 84100 et seq.) A "campaign statement" is defined as "an itemized
report which is prepared on a form prescribed by the [Fair Political Practices]
Commission...." ($ 82006.) Campaign statements are filed on paper at specified times
and places depending on the location and activities of the particular candidate or
committee. ($$ 84200-84215.)
On June 8,2001, the Commission approved revisions to the Form 460 (Recipient
Committee Campaign Statement) to implement legislative changes that were signed into
law during 2000 (Chapter 853, Stats. 2000), as well as Proposition34, which was passed
by the voters in the November 2000 general election. In order to comply fully with the
Act's campaign reporting provisions, candidates and committees must disclose the
information required by the revised Form 460 on their paper reports.
Because changes to the San Francisco Ethics Commission's electronic Form 460
will not be completed in time for the October 10, 2001 campaign filing deadline, those
changes also will not be available to City of Sacramento candidates and committees.
Therefore, you have requested approval to use the modified version the Form 460
approved for use by County and City of San Francisco candidates and committees for
reports due on October 10,2001. The sample form submitted by the Ethics Commission
will allow candidates and committees that file electronically to print the old Form 460 to
which they will manually add Schedules B and H, where appropriate, as contained in the
new Form 460.


Our File No. A-01-231
Page3
The Ethics Commission sample Form 460 complies with the reporting
requirements applicable to city candidates and committees and may be used for paper
filings due on October 10,2001.
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please cali me at (916) 322-5660
ortoll-free at (866) 275-3772.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By:
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
